title: helianthemum niloticum subsp. papillare
intro: Helianthemum niloticum subsp. papillare, also known as the Egyptian rockrose, is a plant species that belongs to the Cistaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Egyptian medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including wound healing, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular protection. Helianthemum niloticum subsp. papillare can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
